tluaIoRead.tlua(48,34): error TLUA2345: Argument of type '"*x"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'LuaReadFormat'.


==== tluaIoRead.tlua (1 errors) ====
    // A failed "*n" stops the read, so the trailing "*a" can be nil at runtime;
    // only the FIRST format can promise a value.
    local n, rest = io.read("*n", "*a");
    local nType: number | nil = n;
    local restType: string | nil = rest;
    
    local wholeFile = io.read("*a");
    local wholeFileType: string = wholeFile;
    
    local defaultLine = io.read();
    local defaultLineType: string | nil = defaultLine;
    
    local bytes = io.read(8);
    local bytesType: string | nil = bytes;
    
    declare f: LuaFile;
    local fileNumber = f.read("*n");
    local fileNumberType: number | nil = fileNumber;
    
    for line in io.lines() do
      local lineType: string = line;
    end
    
    for chunk in io.lines("file", "*a") do
      local chunkType: string = chunk;
    end
    
    for filenameOnly in io.lines("*n") do
      local filenameOnlyType: string = filenameOnly;
    end
    
    declare fmt: LuaReadFormat;
    local x = io.read(fmt);
    local xType: any = x;
    
    // A literal prefix keeps its precision; the runtime-only formats become an
    // open any tail.
    local pn, px = io.read("*n", fmt);
    local pnType: number | nil = pn;
    
    function shadowedIO(): void
      local io = {
        read = function(format: string): boolean return format == "*n"; end,
      };
      local shadowed: boolean = io.read("*n");
    end
    
    local wrong, wrongRest = io.read("*x");
                                     ~~~~
!!! error TLUA2345: Argument of type '"*x"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'LuaReadFormat'.
